L1.area: Statistics data collection, storage, and reporting backend

L2.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where user-controlled input values are directly interpolated into SQL query strings used by statistics data retrieval methods, rather than being properly bound as parameters in prepared statements. An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL to read or modify database contents.

L3.area: Database command layer handling statistics query construction and execution
L3.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where user-controlled identifiers and date range parameters are directly concatenated into SQL query strings used by statistics data retrieval methods, rather than being bound as parameters in prepared statements. An attacker can manipulate entity identifiers or interval values to inject arbitrary SQL and compromise the database.

ground_truth.reason: Multiple methods in the database command layer directly interpolate user-controlled input (entity identifiers, service names, and day interval values) into SQL query strings without using parameterized prepared statements, allowing SQL injection.
